theory.intro: Many real-life problems combine or compare fractions of the same whole. When the denominators match, add or subtract the numerators only.
facts: Same denominator means **same-size parts**, so $\tfrac{a}{d} \pm \tfrac{b}{d} = \tfrac{a \pm b}{d}$.
Keep the **denominator the same** — only the numerators change.
'Altogether' or 'in total' means add; 'left', 'more' or 'difference' means subtract.
steps: Decide whether to add or subtract from the wording.
Combine the numerators over the common denominator.
